children.heading: .—Eligibility Determined by Secretary
children.label: a .—Eligibility Determined by Secretary
children.text: Eligibility of general purpose local governments for assistance under this chapter shall be based on need as determined by the Secretary. The Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register a list of local governments eligible to participate in this program, to be accompanied by a discussion of criteria used in determining eligibility. Criteria shall be based on factors that the Secretary determines are related to deteriorated recreational facilities or systems and physical and economic distress.

children.heading: .—Additional Eligible General Purpose Local Governments
children.label: b .—Additional Eligible General Purpose Local Governments
children.text: In addition to eligible local governments established in accordance with subsection (a), the Secretary may establish eligibility, in accord with the findings and purpose of the Urban Park and Recreation Recovery Act of 1978 (, ), of other general purpose local governments in metropolitan statistical areas as defined by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget.Public Law 95–62592 Stat. 3538

children.heading: Priority Criteria for Project Selection and Approval.—
children.label: c Priority Criteria for Project Selection and Approval.—
B
children.text: condition of existing recreation areas and facilities;

children.label: C
children.text: demonstrated deficiencies in access to neighborhood recreation opportunities, particularly for minority and low- and moderate-income residents;

children.label: D
children.text: public participation in determining rehabilitation or development needs;

children.label: E
children.text: the extent to which a project supports or complements target activities undertaken as part of a local government’s overall community development and urban revitalization program;

children.label: i
children.text: employment opportunities for minorities, youth, and low- and moderate-income residents in the project neighborhood;

children.label: ii
children.text: for participation of neighborhood, nonprofit, or tenant organizations in the proposed rehabilitation activity or in subsequent maintenance, staffing, or supervision of recreation areas and facilities; or

children.label: G
children.text: the amount of State and private support for a project as evidenced by commitments of non-Federal resources to project construction or operation.

children.label: A
children.text: Programs that are targeted to youth who are at the greatest risk of becoming involved in violence and crime.

children.label: B
children.text: Programs that teach important values and life skills, including teamwork, respect, leadership, and self-esteem.

children.label: C
children.text: Programs that offer tutoring, remedial education, mentoring, and counseling in addition to recreation opportunities.

children.label: D
children.text: Programs that offer services during late night or other nonschool hours.

children.label: E
children.text: Programs that demonstrate collaboration between local park and recreation, juvenile justice, law enforcement, and youth social service agencies and nongovernmental entities, including the private sector and community and nonprofit organizations.

children.label: F
children.text: Programs that leverage public or private recreation investments in the form of services, materials, or cash.

children.label: G
children.text: Programs that show the greatest potential of being continued with non-Federal funds or that can serve as models for other communities.

children.heading: .—Limitation of Funds
children.label: d .—Limitation of Funds
children.text: Grants to discretionary applicants under subsection (b) may not be more than 15 percent of the total amount of funds appropriated under this chapter for rehabilitation grants, innovation grants, and recovery action program grants.
